config_file_locations.windows_note: Source 2 uses two config surfaces: a JSON-style video.txt for GPU/display settings (rewritten by in-game video menu), and plaintext .cfg files for console variables (persistent across sessions). autoexec.cfg in the cfg\ directory is executed automatically on game start.

keys.notes: Exclusive fullscreen (1) provides the lowest input latency and enables hardware flip presentation. Borderless windowed (2) is convenient for multi-monitor setups but adds ~1 frame of latency via DWM composition.
Competitive players commonly use 1280x960 or 1024x768 stretched for larger player models and higher FPS. This is a preference setting — for optimisation-only users, match monitor native resolution.

keys.effect: Texture detail / GPU memory usage level: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high.
Overall shader and rendering quality level: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=ultra.
CPU-side simulation quality (ragdolls, debris, particle density): 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high.
keys.notes: CPU level above 1 provides minimal visual benefit in a competitive shooter. Keeping at 0 or 1 reduces CPU overhead in intense firefights.
MSAA above 0 reduces FPS measurably. Competitive players universally use 0. FXAA or no AA is the standard for performance mode.

console_vars.notes: Setting fps_max slightly above monitor refresh rate (e.g. 251 for 240 Hz) keeps the frame pipeline full without uncapped heat/power draw. Unlimited (0) causes GPU thermal throttling during low-complexity frames.

console_vars.effect: In-game FPS counter: 0=off, 1=on, 4=detailed with frame time.

launch_options.effect: Skips the Valve intro video on game launch.
Launches the game process with Windows High CPU priority.
launch_options.notes: May cause audio dropouts or system instability. Only beneficial on CPUs with many background processes. Not recommended.

launch_options.effect: Disables joystick/controller subsystem. Reduces startup time if no controller is used.
Explicitly executes autoexec.cfg on launch. Required if autoexec.cfg is not being loaded automatically.
